cases.title: Assigned Lecturer opens Scheduled session and enables attendance window
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned to the target class section.
A class session exists in Scheduled state with enrolled students.
No openedAt timestamp is set on the session.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with Lecturer Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard {data, meta, error} envelope.
Assert data.state is Open, openedAt is non-null ISO-8601 UTC, and data.qr contains expiresAt and qrPayload.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and verify tokenState is Valid with matching session binding.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as an enrolled student using the issued QR token and confirm check-in is not rejected with SessionNotOpen.
cases.expected: WF-01 / E2E-01 happy path: session transitions Scheduled to Open, openedAt and actor are recorded, QR rotation starts, and student check-in is no longer blocked by BR-01 SessionNotOpen.

cases.title: Testing-plan session open/close row: open records metadata and gates check-in by state
preconditions: Lecturer assigned to section is authenticated.
Session fixture is in Scheduled state per testing-plan §3.1 Session open/close coverage block.
Enrolled student account and valid QR token fixture are available after open.
steps: While session is Scheduled, submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student and record SessionNotOpen rejection.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Assert response data.state Open with openedAt and qr metadata per AC-01 Given/When/Then.
Re-submit POST /v1/check-ins with current QR token and confirm outcome is not SessionNotOpen.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and assert data.state is Open with live roster counts.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan Session open/close row: pre-open check-in fails with SessionNotOpen (BR-01); open succeeds with metadata; post-open check-in and roster reflect Open state.

cases.title: M03 Session Lifecycle atomically persists Open state with openedAt and openedByUserId
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for class_sessions table.
A Scheduled session row exists with known sessionId and assigned lecturerUserId on the parent section.
Lecturer auth context matches the section assignment.
steps: Invoke M03 openSession(sessionId, actor) or POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open through API service layer.
Query class_sessions table and assert state = Open, opened_at is non-null, and opened_by_user_id equals the Lecturer actor.
Invoke getSessionState(sessionId) and assert Open with no closedAt timestamp.
Verify a QRSessionToken row or equivalent is created with Valid state bound to the session per SM-02 rotation hook.
cases.expected: M03 module + repository integration commits state Open, openedAt, and openedByUserId atomically before returning QR metadata; no partial open state without actor/timestamp.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open returns 200 envelope with Open payload and QR preview
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Target session is in Scheduled state.
Request includes Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers per API design §2.2.
steps: Send POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with optional empty body.
Validate HTTP 200 and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error} with error null.
Assert data contains classSessionId, state Open, openedAt, and qr object with expiresAt and qrPayload.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp fields.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with standard envelope; data reflects Open session with openedAt and QR preview metadata per API design §4.3.

cases.title: Only Scheduled pre-state transitions to Open; Open Closed and Cancelled are rejected
preconditions: Session fixtures exist in Scheduled, Open, Closed, and Cancelled states.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is available for all attempts.
steps: Record beforeState Scheduled and invoke open for Scheduled session; record afterState Open.
Attempt open on session already in Open state.
Attempt open on Closed session.
Attempt open on Cancelled session.
Query persistence after each rejected attempt and confirm state unchanged.
cases.expected: beforeState Scheduled transitions to afterState Open; Open, Closed, and Cancelled pre-states reject with invalid-transition behavior (409 InvalidSessionTransition) and no illegal state persistence per SM-01.

cases.title: Non-assigned and non-lecturer roles are denied SessionControl.execute open
preconditions: Student, ITAdmin, and Lecturer-not-assigned-to-section test accounts exist with valid tokens.
A Scheduled session exists in a section the out-of-scope Lecturer is not assigned to.
AcademicAdmin token is available as positive control for override permission.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as Student.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as out-of-scope Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code (expect 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope) for each denied request.
Query class_sessions table to confirm session state remains Scheduled.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as AcademicAdmin and assert override succeeds per permission matrix §3.1.
cases.expected: Student, ITAdmin, and out-of-scope Lecturer receive 403 without state change; AcademicAdmin override may open per roles doc; assigned Lecturer remains primary actor for FR-07.

cases.title: Open on Cancelled or Closed session returns InvalidSessionTransition
preconditions: Cancelled and Closed session fixtures exist with known sessionIds.
Assigned Lecturer and AcademicAdmin tokens are available.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{cancelledSessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{closedSessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each response.
Verify class_sessions rows remain Cancelled and Closed respectively with no openedAt set.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 409 with error.code InvalidSessionTransition for Cancelled and Closed targets; FR-07 explicitly rejects open when session is Cancelled or already Closed.

cases.title: Lecturer opens session with optional roomId override for timetable change
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Scheduled session exists with default room on the section.
An alternate roomId with valid room metadata exists for override.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with body {"roomId": "{alternateRoomId}"} per API design §4.3 optional room override.
Validate HTTP 200 and data.state Open.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions?classSectionId={sectionId} and locate the opened session.
Assert session roomId reflects the override while openedAt and QR metadata are present.
cases.expected: Alternate branch succeeds: session opens with room override applied for the session instance; attendance window opens normally with updated room binding.

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate open requests are idempotent and return consistent Open state
preconditions: Scheduled session exists with known sessionId.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with same Idempotency-Key.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is established.
steps: Submit two or more parallel open requests with identical Idempotency-Key and Lecturer token.
Collect HTTP status codes and response data.state for all responses.
Query class_sessions table and assert exactly one Open transition with single openedAt/openedByUserId.
Verify no duplicate QR token issuance anomalies beyond expected rotation semantics.
cases.expected: All replayed open commands return consistent Open summary; persistence shows single legal transition per API idempotency rules §7.1; no duplicate side effects.

cases.title: Lecturer authenticates via login then opens assigned session
preconditions: Lecturer credentials exist for POST /v1/auth/login.
A Scheduled session exists in a section assigned to that Lecturer.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with Lecturer credentials and capture accessToken and roles from HTTP 200 envelope.
Send GET /v1/me with bearer token and assert Lecturer identity and assigned section scope.
Send POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with bearer token and Idempotency-Key.
Assert open returns HTTP 200 with data.state Open within authenticated lecturer scope.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken; GET /v1/me returns Lecturer identity; subsequent open succeeds in Open state for assigned session only.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance reflects Open state after open
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Scheduled session with enrollments exists.
Session has not yet been opened.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open and capture openedAt from response.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization.
Validate HTTP 200 and envelope shape.
Assert data.state is Open, counts object includes present/late/pending/rejectedAttempts keys, and rows array reflects enrolled roster shape per API design §4.5.
cases.expected: Attendance roster endpoint returns HTTP 200 with data.state Open and live roster payload shape immediately after FR-07 open; enables FR-19 realtime dashboard prerequisite.

cases.title: Lecturer PG-04/PG-05 journey opens Scheduled session and shows Open badge with QR panel
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
Assigned section has at least one Scheduled session visible on PG-04 session list.
Enrollments exist for the section.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions (PG-04) and locate a Scheduled session row with Scheduled status badge.
Select the session and navigate to session detail / QR display (PG-05).
Tap or click Open Attendance (FRM-09) and observe loading then success feedback.
Verify session badge transitions to Open, openedAt indicator appears, and QR panel renders with countdown or refresh indicator.
Confirm Open CTA is disabled or hidden and close/view-roster actions become available per UI states §2.
cases.expected: Browser journey completes FLOW-05: UI transitions Scheduled to Open with openedAt metadata, active QR display, and state-appropriate control gating without full page reload.
